description: Thermal tolerance limits for *Acropora* coral species under stable conditions.
implication: Any experimental setup or simulation involving these species must maintain water temperatures strictly between 15°C and 30°C to remain ecologically viable.

description: Minimum depth required for successful establishment of the target habitat structure.
implication: Field or simulation designs must define the experimental area within this 10m to 50m depth range to simulate realistic habitat conditions for the species.

description: Thermal tolerance limits for *Acropora millepora* coral growth under stable conditions.
implication: Any simulation or experiment modeling *A. millepora* must maintain water temperatures strictly between 16°C and 29°C to remain ecologically viable.

description: Minimum dissolved oxygen required to support the metabolic rate of the coral microbiome.
implication: The simulation must ensure that flow rates are sufficient to maintain dissolved oxygen levels above 5.5 mg/L across the simulated depth profile to prevent ecosystem collapse.

description: The maximum sustainable carrying capacity (K) for target fish populations based on localized resource density.
implication: The simulation results for the population size must not exceed 4500 individuals under the specified resource dependency to reflect established ecological carrying capacity.

description: Minimum light penetration depth required for sustained primary production in the target habitat.
implication: Field or simulation designs must define the experimental area within this depth range (5m) to ensure that photosynthetic organisms receive sufficient light for survival.

description: Minimum metabolic rate required for a fish species to maintain basal survival rates.
implication: Any simulation must ensure that the calculated food supply provides at least 400 kcal per day to meet the minimum metabolic demands of the simulated population.
